name: Input Prompt
description: The bottom-anchored message input bar of the main conversation panel. Borderless single-line input with a dim placeholder prompt and a blinking block cursor █. No visible input box chrome — the prompt reads as a continuation of the terminal surface. On focus the cursor blink accelerates; on submit the line clears and the message moves into the conversation stream above.

anatomy.description: Full-width row, padding var(--space-2) var(--space-3). Top border 1px var(--color-divider) separating from conversation area above. Background var(--color-bg-primary)
Dim prompt text, var(--text-base), color var(--color-text-muted)
Blinking █ block, color var(--color-accent-bright). CSS animation: opacity 1→0 over 1s, steps(2). Accelerates to 0.5s on focus
When mode=command, a leading '/' in var(--color-accent) precedes the input

structurePatterns: display: flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2)
Input element: background transparent; border: none; outline: none; color var(--color-text-primary); flex: 1
usageHints: Anchor to the bottom of the conversation panel with position: sticky
Enter submits; Shift+Enter inserts newline (rare in pure TUI mode)
Up-arrow recalls previous inputs from session history
doNotInvent: Never add a visible input border or focus ring — the cursor blink IS the focus indicator
Never add a send button; submission is keyboard-only (Enter)
Do not add placeholder icons or microphones; text-only TUI
